name: ATAC-seq
title: ATAC-seq

name: CAPP-seq
title: CAPP-seq
description: Cancer Personalized Profiling by deep sequencing (CAPP-seq) is a novel blood-based assay that uses next-generating sequencing to quantitate circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA)

name: CUT&RUN
title: CUT&RUN
description: Cleavage Under Targets and Release Using Nuclease (CUT&RUN) is a chromatin profiling strategy in which antibody-targeted controlled cleavage by micrococcal nuclease releases specific protein-DNA complexes into the supernatant for paired-end DNA sequencing. (doi:10.7554/eLife.21856)
rop_description: Cohort-scoped batch identifier for the assay run. Required for batch-effect correction in cross-cohort meta-analysis. Granularity is study-defined (sequencing run, plate, library prep batch) but must be consistent within a collection.

name: ChIP-seq
title: ChIP-seq
description: Chromatin immuno-precipitation followed by sequencing

name: ERR bisulfite sequencing
title: ERR bisulfite sequencing
description: Enhanced reduced representation bisulfite sequencing (ERRBS)

name: HI-C
title: HI-C
description: Chromatin interactions detected by HI-C protocol

name: ISO-seq
title: ISO-seq
description: Full isoform sequencing
rop_description: Sequential visit identifier within the study; 0 = baseline/screening visit.

name: NOMe-seq
title: NOMe-seq
description: Nucleosome Occupancy and Methylome Sequencing

name: RNA array
title: RNA array
description: RNA measurements captured by array technology

name: RNA-seq
title: RNA-seq
description: Bulk RNA sequencing
rop_description: Sequential visit identifier within the study; 0 = baseline/screening visit.

name: SNP array
title: SNP array
description: SNP measurements captured by array technology

name: SaferSeqS
title: SaferSeqS
description: A blood-based assay that evaluates mutations in circulating tumor DNA and chromosomal copy number changes to detect minimal residual disease (MRD) with high specificity towards tumor type.

name: Sanger sequencing
title: Sanger sequencing
description: A DNA sequencing technique in which a mixture of deoxynucleosidetriphosphates (dNTPs) and chain-terminating dNTPs, which are radioactively or fluorescently labeled, are combined within the reaction mixture. Once the reaction is complete, the DNA strands are separated by size, and the labeled chain terminating dNTPs can be read in sequence by the investigator or by a machine.

name: T cell receptor repertoire sequencing
title: T cell receptor repertoire sequencing
description: A sequencing assay that determines the sequences of DNA or RNA molecules that encode the repertoire of T cell receptors within an input sample.

name: bisulfite sequencing
title: bisulfite sequencing
description: Methylation data captured by sequencing of DNA treated by a bisulfite-based chemical process

name: jumping library
title: jumping library
description: Jumping libraries are created to bypass difficult to align/map regions, such as those containing repetitive DNA sequences. Briefly, the DNA of interest is identified, cut into fragments either with restriction enzymes or by shearing.  The size-selected fragments are ligated to adapters for bead-capture and circularized. After bead-capture, the DNA is linearized via restriction enzymes, and can be sequenced using adapter primers facing in outward [reverse/forward (RF)] directions.  These library inserts are considered jumping because the ends originate from distal genomic DNA sequences and are ligated adjacent to one another during circularization.
rop_description: Library prep, capture, or chemistry kit used. Free text capturing what was actually recorded. Examples: 'TruSeq Stranded mRNA', 'Single Cell 3' v3.1', 'Twist Comprehensive Exome', 'Infinium MethylationEPIC v2.0'.

name: lncRNA-seq
title: lncRNA-seq
description: Long non-coding RNA measurements collected from RNA-Seq experiments

name: methylation array
title: methylation array
description: Methylation data captured by array technology

name: miRNA array
title: miRNA array
description: microRNA measurements captured by array technology

name: miRNA-seq
title: miRNA-seq
description: Small RNA measurements collected from RNA sequencing experiments

name: next-generation sequencing
title: next-generation sequencing
description: High-throughput DNA sequencing methods that allow massively parallel sequencing of millions of DNA fragments simultaneously.

name: next generation targeted sequencing
title: next generation targeted sequencing
description: A type of next generation sequencing in which specific genes or portions of genes are targeted for sequencing using amplicon-based workflow.
aliases: Targeted NGS|Targeted NGS (sequencing of selected regions of the genome either via targeted capture or amplification)|Targeted Sequencing

name: oxBS-seq
title: oxBS-seq
description: Oxidative bisulfite sequencing (oxBS-Seq) to map 5-methylcytosine and 5-hydroxymethylcytosine

name: ribo-seq
title: ribo-seq
description: Ribosome profiling (Ribo-Seq).

name: scCGI-seq
title: scCGI-seq
description: A method for genome-wide CpG island (CGI) methylation sequencing for single cells (scCGI-seq), combining methylation-sensitive restriction enzyme digestion and multiple displacement amplification for selective detection of methylated CGIs

name: single cell ATAC-seq
title: single cell ATAC-seq
description: A molecular genetic technique where DNA is isolated from single cell (sc) samples and amplified to create a genomic library. Then the library is subjected to ATAC-seq, which isolates and sequences regions rich in open chromatin.

name: single-cell RNA-seq
title: single-cell RNA-seq
description: A procedure that can determine the nucleotide sequence for all of the RNA transcripts in an amplified nucleotide sample that was derived from a single cell.
aliases: Single Cell RNA Sequencing|Single Cell Transcriptome Sequencing|scRNA-Seq

name: single-nucleus RNA-seq
title: single-nucleus RNA-seq
description: A method for quantifying the transcriptome of individual cells, in which transcripts isolated from single nuclei are subjected to high-throughput sequencing and mapping to a reference genome.

name: spatial transcriptomics
title: spatial transcriptomics
description: assay that allows visualization and quantitative analysis of the transcriptome with spatial resolution in individual tissue sections

name: targeted exome sequencing
title: targeted exome sequencing
description: Also known as Clinical Exome Sequencing, Targeted Exome Sequencing is a subset of WES as it covers a limited number of genes, typically 3000 to 6000 genes.

name: whole exome sequencing
title: whole exome sequencing
description: A procedure that can determine the DNA sequence for all of the exons in an individual.

name: whole genome sequencing
title: whole genome sequencing
description: Laboratory technique to sequence the complete DNA sequence of an organism's genome at a single time

name: 3D confocal imaging
title: 3D confocal imaging
description: Confocal microscopy with 3D reconstruction of the sample tissue.
rop_description: Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) conformance level. BIDS is the de facto standard for organizing neuroimaging datasets and is required for many community pipelines (fMRIPrep, QSIPrep, MRIQC). Recording conformance level helps consumers know whether they can run BIDS-Apps directly or need a pre-processing step.

name: 3D confocal imaging
title: 3D confocal imaging
description: Confocal microscopy with 3D reconstruction of the sample tissue.
rop_description: Imaging modality. Determines which other anchors apply: MRI brings field strength and sequence type; PET brings tracer and injected dose; CT brings tube voltage; OCT brings axial resolution; ultrasound brings transducer frequency. DICOM tag (0008,0060) maps directly here.

name: 3D confocal imaging
title: 3D confocal imaging
description: Confocal microscopy with 3D reconstruction of the sample tissue.
rop_description: Quality control status from the cohort's QC pipeline. Records whether this scan passed automated and/or manual QC. JSON object {status, qc_pipeline, qc_pipeline_version, qc_notes, manual_review}. Common QC pipelines: MRIQC for structural MRI, FMRIPREP for fMRI, dMRIQC for diffusion. Allows downstream consumers to filter out low-quality scans without re-running QC.

name: 3D electron microscopy
title: 3D electron microscopy
description: Three-dimensional (3D) reconstruction of single, transparent objects from a collection of projection images recorded with a transmission electron microscope. It offers the opportunity to obtain 3D information on structural cellular arrangements with a high resolution.

name: 3D imaging
title: 3D imaging
description: Technique to produce 3D images to visualize important structures in great detail. To produce 3D images, many scans are made, and then combined by computers to produce a 3D model, which can then be manipulated. 3D ultrasounds are produced using a somewhat similar technique.
aliases: 3-Dimensional Imaging|Medical Imaging, Three Dimensional|Three Dimensional Imaging

name: CODEX
title: CODEX
description: CODEX imaging.

name: DNA optical mapping
title: DNA optical mapping
description: Fluorescent imaging of linearly extended DNA molecules to probe information patterns along the molecules

name: Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization
title: Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization
description: A physical mapping approach that uses fluorescent tags to detect hybridization of probes within metaphase chromosomes or less condensed somatic interphase chromatin.  This technique can be used for identification of chromosomal abnormalities and for gene mapping.

name: Magnetization-Prepared Rapid Gradient Echo MRI
title: Magnetization-Prepared Rapid Gradient Echo MRI
description: A magnetic resonance imaging modality that offers rapid imaging time, easy reconstruction of any plane, and three-dimensional surface contour rendering with cut away postprocessing.  Especially useful for imaging brain, MP-RAGE captures high tissue contrast and provides high spatial resolution with whole brain coverage in a short scan time.

name: SUSHI
title: SUSHI
description: Fast super-resolution method that exploits sparsity in the underlying vasculature and statistical independence within the measured signals to detect slow-flowing blood, facilitating noninvasive perfusion measurements.

name: atomic force microscopy
title: atomic force microscopy
description: Microscopy which uses a sharp spike (known as a 'tip') mounted on the end of a cantilever to scan the surface of the specimen

name: autoradiography
title: autoradiography
description: A radioactivity detection technique using X-ray film to visualize molecules or fragments of molecules that have been radioactively labeled.

name: brightfield microscopy
title: brightfield microscopy
description: Microscopy where the specimen is illuminated with light transmitted from a source on the opposite side of the specimen from the objective

name: live imaging
title: live imaging
description: Imaging of live cells, tissues, or organisms over time to observe dynamic biological processes and behavior.
rop_description: Imaging modality. Determines which other anchors apply: MRI brings field strength and sequence type; PET brings tracer and injected dose; CT brings tube voltage; OCT brings axial resolution; ultrasound brings transducer frequency. DICOM tag (0008,0060) maps directly here.

name: live imaging
title: live imaging
description: Imaging of live cells, tissues, or organisms over time to observe dynamic biological processes and behavior.
rop_description: Scanner manufacturer. DICOM tag (0008,0070). Critical for cross-cohort harmonization because Siemens / GE / Philips / Canon scanners produce systematically different intensity distributions even with nominally identical sequences. Multi-site studies typically include manufacturer as a covariate.

name: live imaging
title: live imaging
description: Imaging of live cells, tissues, or organisms over time to observe dynamic biological processes and behavior.
rop_description: Specific scanner model. DICOM tag (0008,1090). Examples: 'MAGNETOM Prisma' (Siemens 3T research scanner), 'SIGNA Premier' (GE 3T), 'Ingenia Elition' (Philips 3T), 'Discovery MI' (GE PET/CT). Distinct from manufacturer because batch effects often track model rather than vendor.

name: histology
title: histology
description: Microscopic examination of stained tissue sections to evaluate tissue architecture and cellular morphology.

name: confocal microscopy
title: confocal microscopy
description: Confocal microscopy has advantages over widefield optical microscopy, including the ability to eliminate or reduce background information away from the focal plane and collect serial optical sections from thick specimens.  It uses point illumination and a spatial pinhole to eliminate out-of-focus light in specimens that are thicker than the focal plane.

name: conventional MRI
title: conventional MRI
description: Magnetic resonance imaging using standard protocols for high resolution structural and anatomic characterization, including T1 weighted, T2 weighted, fluid attenuated inversion recovery (FLAIR), and gadolinium-enhanced sequences. [  NCI  ]
rop_description: Static magnetic field strength in Tesla. DICOM tag (0018,0087). Required for MRI/fMRI/dMRI/MRS modalities; null otherwise. Cross-cohort meta-analysis typically stratifies by field strength because 1.5T, 3T, and 7T scanners produce systematically different SNR, contrast, and susceptibility artifacts.

name: diffusion MRI
title: diffusion MRI
description: MRI method that measure the diffusion of water in the tissue, rather than the content of water as measured in conventional MRI.

name: fluorescence microscopy assay
title: fluorescence microscopy assay
description: Uses fluorescent labels to visualize and quantify specific cellular components, proteins, or biological processes under a fluorescence microscope by detecting the emitted light when the fluorophores are excited by specific wavelengths.

name: functional MRI
title: functional MRI
description: The principle of functional MRI imaging is to take a series of images of an organ in quick succession and to statistically analyze the images for differences among them. Most commonly used in studies of brain function. [  NCI  ]
rop_description: Static magnetic field strength in Tesla. DICOM tag (0018,0087). Required for MRI/fMRI/dMRI/MRS modalities; null otherwise. Cross-cohort meta-analysis typically stratifies by field strength because 1.5T, 3T, and 7T scanners produce systematically different SNR, contrast, and susceptibility artifacts.

name: gel filtration chromatography
title: gel filtration chromatography
description: 'Gel filtration chromatography (synonym: gel permeation chromatography - GPC) is a type of size-exclusion chromatography (SEC), that separates analytes on the basis of size, typically in organic solvents.  The technique is often used for the analysis of polymers.'

name: high content screen
title: high content screen
description: An image analysis technique combining automated fluorescence microscopy with multi-parameter quantitative image analysis for the large scale study of cells (cellomics).
rop_description: Whether the loadings are per-individual (suitable as covariates), per-feature (suitable for back-projection), or both. Critical because consumers need different file formats for different uses.

name: high frequency ultrasound
title: high frequency ultrasound
description: High frequency ultrasound (HFUS) is an imaging modality that can give greater resolution of surface changes in skin (review in PMID:24583666)

name: immunocytochemistry
title: immunocytochemistry
description: The branch of immunochemistry dealing with cells and cellular activity; the application of immunochemical techniques to cytochemistry.

name: immunofluorescence
title: immunofluorescence
description: An immunological procedure in which the antibodies are coupled with molecules which fluoresce under ultra violet (UV) light. This makes them particularly suitable for detection of specific antigens in tissues or on cells.
aliases: FLUORESCENT IMMUNOASSAY|Fluorescent Antibody Assay|Fluorescent Antibody Procedure|Immunofluorescence Assay|Immunologic, Immunofluorescence

name: immunohistochemistry
title: immunohistochemistry
description: An immunostaining assay to detect and potentially localize antigens within the cells of a tissue section

name: in vivo bioluminescence
title: in vivo bioluminescence
description: An imaging assay that allows detection of bioluminescence from a living organism or organisms.

name: laser speckle imaging
title: laser speckle imaging
description: A noninvasive, non-scanning optical imaging technique that provides full-field visualization of blood flow to the tissue being imaged, which provides information about tissue perfusion and the efficiency of disease treatment.
rop_description: Contrast agent administered, when applicable. JSON object {agent_name, dose_mL, dose_mmol_per_kg, administration_route, timing_relative_to_scan_min}. Common in MRI (gadolinium-based: Gadovist, Dotarem, Magnevist) and CT (iodinated). Null for native-contrast scans.

name: magnetic resonance angiography
title: magnetic resonance angiography
description: Angiography using magnetic resonance imaging.

name: magnetic resonance spectroscopy
title: magnetic resonance spectroscopy
description: Detection and measurement of the resonant spectra of molecular species in a tissue or sample.
aliases: NMR Spectroscopy|NUCLEAR MAGNETIC RESONANCE SPECTROSCOPY|Spectroscopy, MR|Spectroscopy, Nuclear Magnetic Resonance

name: optical coherence tomography
title: optical coherence tomography
description: Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) combines the principles of ultrasound with the imaging performance of a microscope.  OCT uses infrared light waves that reflect off the internal microstructure within the biological tissues.  The frequencies and bandwidths of infrared light are orders of magnitude higher than medical ultrasound signals, resulting in greatly increased image resolution, 8-25 times greater than any existing modality.  In addition to providing high-level resolutions for the evaluation of microanatomic structures OCT is also able to provide information regarding tissue composition. OCT is most widely used in ophthalmology.

name: optical tomography
title: optical tomography
description: Optical tomography is a form of computed tomography that creates a digital volumetric model of an object by reconstructing images made from light transmitted and scattered through an object. Subtypes are diffuse optical tomography, time-of-flight diffuse optical tomography, fluorescence molecular tomography, confocal diffuse tomography, optical coherence tomography.

name: phase-contrast microscopy
title: phase-contrast microscopy
description: A simple non-quantitative form of interference microscopy of great utility in visualising live cells. Small differences in optical path length due to differences in refractive index and thickness of structures are visualised as differences in light intensity.

name: photograph
title: photograph
description: An image recorded by a camera.

name: positron emission tomography
title: positron emission tomography
description: A technique for measuring the gamma radiation produced by collisions of electrons and positrons (anti-electrons) within living tissue.
aliases: Medical Imaging, Positron Emission Tomography|PET|PET SCAN|PT|Positron emission tomography (procedure)|proton magnetic resonance spectroscopic imaging
rop_description: Static magnetic field strength in Tesla. DICOM tag (0018,0087). Required for MRI/fMRI/dMRI/MRS modalities; null otherwise. Cross-cohort meta-analysis typically stratifies by field strength because 1.5T, 3T, and 7T scanners produce systematically different SNR, contrast, and susceptibility artifacts.

name: spatial frequency domain imaging
title: spatial frequency domain imaging
description: Spatial Frequency Domain Imaging (SFDI) is a non-contact, depth-varying and wide-field optical imaging technique for measuring optical properties in a wide field-of-view on a pixel-by-pixel basis. Relevant applications include including burn assessment, skin tissue evaluation, tumor tissue detection, brain tissue monitoring.

name: traction force microscopy
title: traction force microscopy
description: An experimental method for determining the tractions on the surface of a biological cell by obtaining measurements of the surrounding displacement field within an in vitro extracellular matrix (ECM)

name: transcranial doppler ultrasonography
title: transcranial doppler ultrasonography
description: A diagnostic technique that uses pulsed Doppler ultrasound to measure the velocity of blood flow through the major blood vessels of the brain.

name: western blot
title: western blot
description: A multistep process in which a mixture of proteins is separated by gel electrophoresis

name: FIA-MSMS
title: FIA-MSMS
description: Flow injection analysis - tandem mass spectrometer

name: FTIR spectroscopy
title: FTIR spectroscopy
description: Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy is a technique used to obtain an infrared spectrum of absorption or emission of a solid, liquid or gas.  An FTIR spectrometer simultaneously collects high-resolution spectral data over a wide spectral range.
aliases: FTIR spectrometry|FTIR|Fourier transform infra-red absorption spectroscopy|Fourier transform infrared spectrometry|Fourier transform infrared (FT-IR)|Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy

name: MIB/MS
title: MIB/MS
description: Kinomics assay using multiplexed kinase inhibitor beads and mass spectrometry (MIB/MS)

name: MudPIT
title: MudPIT
description: MudPIT is a method for rapid and large-scale protein identification by multidimensional liquid chromatography associated with tandem mass spectrometry

name: RPPA
title: RPPA
description: Reverse phase protein array (RPPA) is a an assay that measures multiple protein expression levels in a large number of biological samples simultaneously using high quality antibodies

name: TMT quantitation
title: TMT quantitation
description: An isobaric labeling technique that uses tags containing four regions with the same total molecular weights and structure, so that during chromatographic or electrophoretic separation and in single MS mode, molecules labelled with different tags are indistinguishable.

name: high-performance liquid chromatography/tandem mass spectrometry
title: high-performance liquid chromatography/tandem mass spectrometry
description: HPLC-MSMS is an analytical technique wherein high performance liquid chromatography is coupled to tandem mass spectrometry in order to separate, identify, and quantify substances in a sample.

name: label free mass spectrometry
title: label free mass spectrometry
description: A mass spectrometry assay that allows for measurement for endogenous targets in live cell assays, and eliminates the need for tags, dyes, or specialized reagents or engineered cells.

name: liquid chromatography-electrochemical detection
title: liquid chromatography-electrochemical detection
description: A liquid chromatography method that is sensitive to compounds which can be either reduced or oxidised. The mobile phase passes directly over the working electrode, which is set to the specific potential required for oxidation or reduction. The current produced is then measured.

name: liquid chromatography/mass spectrometry
title: liquid chromatography/mass spectrometry
description: LC-MS is a hyphenated technique, combining the separation power of liquid chromatography (LC), an analytical chromatographic technique for separating ions or molecules dissolved in a solvent, with the detection power of mass spectrometry(MS), a technique to separate gas phase ions according their m/z (mass to charge ratio) value. Used for drug screening, pharmacology studies, environmental analyses and forensics.

name: liquid chromatography/tandem mass spectrometry
title: liquid chromatography/tandem mass spectrometry
description: LC-MSMS is an analytical technique wherein liquid chromatography is coupled to tandem mass spectrometry in order to separate, identify, and quantify substances in a sample.

name: mass spectrometry
title: mass spectrometry
description: A generic mass spectrometry assay that allows identification and amount of peptide or protein materials.

name: proximity extension assay
title: proximity extension assay
description: Olink's Proximity Extension Assay (PEA) combines specificity and scalability to enable high-throughput, multiplex protein biomarker analysis.

name: SomaScan
title: SomaScan
description: Aptamer-based proteomics assay using SOMAmer reagents (modified DNA aptamers) to quantify thousands of proteins simultaneously from small sample volumes.

name: ultra high-performance liquid chromatography/tandem mass spectrometry
title: ultra high-performance liquid chromatography/tandem mass spectrometry
description: UPLC-MSMS is an analytical technique wherein ultra-high performance liquid chromatography is coupled to tandem mass spectrometry in order to separate, identify, and quantify substances in a sample.

name: AlgometRx Nociometer
title: AlgometRx Nociometer
description: Assay that can selectively measure the sensitivity of three different nerve fiber types. An overall score can be derived to indicate the type of pain a person is experiencing and can be used to monitor how well a patient responds to treatment of pain.

name: quantitative sensory testing
title: quantitative sensory testing
description: A battery of psychophysical tests to assess sensory nerve function and pain perception across modalities (e.g., thermal, mechanical, vibration) and quantify sensory thresholds.

name: Child Behavior Checklist for Ages 1.5-5
title: Child Behavior Checklist for Ages 1.5-5
description: "A standardized measure designed to detect behavioral and emotional problems in children 1.5-5 years old.  The forms obtain parents', daycare providers', and teachers' ratings of 99 problem items plus descriptions of problems, disabilities,  what concerns respondents most about the child, and the best things about the child. [ NCI ]".

name: Child Behavior Checklist for Ages 6-18
title: Child Behavior Checklist for Ages 6-18
description: "A standardized measure designed to detect behavioral and emotional problems in children and adolescents.  It is completed by the parent/caretaker who spends the most time with the child and provides ratings for 20 competence and 120 problem items. [ NCI ]"

name: Children's Dermatology Life Quality Index Questionnaire
title: Children's Dermatology Life Quality Index Questionnaire
description: A standardized rating scale originally developed by Lewis-Jones and Finlay in 1995. This instrument is used to make quality of life assessment in children with skin conditions. [ NCI ]

name: Corsi blocks
title: Corsi blocks
description: A visuospatial counterpart to the verbal-memory span task (Milner, 1971). Over the years, it has frequently been used to assess visuospatial short-term memory performance in adults  (e.g. Smyth & Scholey, 1992), children (e.g. Orsini, Schiappa, & Grossi, 1981), and patients with neuropsychological deficits.

name: FACE-Q Appearance-related Distress
title: FACE-Q Appearance-related Distress
description: This 8-item scale measures appearance-related distress in people seeking cosmetic treatments for the body or the face.  Item ask someone to agree/disagree with statements about feelings (e.g., unhappy, stressed, down) and behaviors, such as avoiding being around people.

name: Focus group
title: Focus group
description: A small, usually diverse group of people whose response to something is studied to determine the response that can be expected from a larger population.  It is used especially in market research and political analysis.
rop_description: Identifier for monozygotic (identical) twin or higher-order MZ multiple grouping. All MZ multiples sharing this identifier are genetically identical and should be treated as a single genetic unit for IBD-based and variance-component analyses while remaining distinct for environmental and longitudinal analyses. Null for non-twins and dizygotic twins.

name: Focus group
title: Focus group
description: A small, usually diverse group of people whose response to something is studied to determine the response that can be expected from a larger population.  It is used especially in market research and political analysis.
rop_description: Identifier for dizygotic (fraternal) twin or higher-order DZ multiple grouping. DZ multiples share an average of 50% of genome IBD (like full siblings) but were gestated together and share environmental exposures distinguishing them from non-twin siblings.

name: Interview
title: Interview
description: A conversation with an individual regarding his or her background and other personal and professional details, opinions on specific subjects posed by the interviewer, etc.

name: NIH Toolbox
title: NIH Toolbox
description: A comprehensive set of neuro-behavioral measures that assess cognitive, emotional, sensory, and motor functions"

name: PROMIS Cognitive Function
title: PROMIS Cognitive Function
description: The PROMIS Cognitive Function and Cognitive Function Abilities Subset item banks assess patient-perceived cognitive deficits.

name: Riccardi and Ablon scales
title: Riccardi and Ablon scales
description: The Riccardi and Ablon scales score severity and visibility of disease, respectively, and are commonly used as a combined assessment for NF1 patients in clinical settings. See https://doi.org/10.1001/archderm.137.11.1421 for example usage.

name: Skindex-16
title: Skindex-16
description: A validated measure of the effects of skin diseases on quality of life that [is] suitable for use in research about patients' experiences of illness and its treatment

name: Social Responsiveness Scale
title: Social Responsiveness Scale
description: The Social Responsiveness Scale (SRS; Constantino et al., 2003) is a brief assessment tool for measuring autism traits.

name: Social Responsiveness Scale, Second Edition
title: Social Responsiveness Scale, Second Edition
description: Identifies the presence and severity of social impairment within the autism spectrum and differentiates it from that which occurs in other disorders.

name: Von Frey test
title: Von Frey test
description: Assay applying electrical stimulus to assess pain in rodents.

name: actigraphy
title: actigraphy
description: Use of a portable device (actigraph) to study sleep-wake patterns and circadian rhythms by assessing movement.

name: active avoidance learning behavior assay
title: active avoidance learning behavior assay
description: A behavioral assay devised to measure active avoidance learning behavior

name: auditory brainstem response
title: auditory brainstem response
description: ABR is a gold standard for evaluating hearing in mice. A non-invasive method that measures the electrical activity generated by the auditory nerve and brainstem in response to sound stimuli. Electrodes are placed on the scalp of the mouse, and sound is delivered through a speaker or earphone.

name: blood chemistry measurement
title: blood chemistry measurement
description: The determination of the measured concentrations of chemical constituents of the blood by assay in a clinical laboratory.
aliases: Blood Chemistry Evaluation|Blood Chemistry Study|Blood Chemistry Test

name: body size trait measurement
title: body size trait measurement
description: Any measurable or observable characteristic related to the overall physical magnitude of an organism.

name: cNF-Skindex
title: cNF-Skindex
description: The cNF-Skindex, validated in a French population, specifically assesses the cNF-related QOL.

name: clinical data
title: clinical data
description: Data pertaining to the medical well-being or status of a patient. Category also includes clinical reports and individual patient data.
rop_description: OMOP CDM domain classification of the clinical concept. Determines which downstream tables and analyses apply: a Condition concept lands in OMOP CONDITION_OCCURRENCE, a Measurement concept in MEASUREMENT, a Drug in DRUG_EXPOSURE, etc. RoP follows OMOP's domain enumeration as the controlling vocabulary for clinical concept categorization.

name: clinical data
title: clinical data
description: Data pertaining to the medical well-being or status of a patient. Category also includes clinical reports and individual patient data.
rop_description: OMOP concept_id — the canonical pivot identifier across vocabularies. Two clinical concept rows referencing the same standard_code are semantically equivalent regardless of source vocabulary. Numeric integer; 0 indicates unmapped.

name: clinical data
title: clinical data
description: Data pertaining to the medical well-being or status of a patient. Category also includes clinical reports and individual patient data.
rop_description: Preferred concept name in the standard vocabulary (OMOP concept.concept_name). Examples: 'Type 2 diabetes mellitus', 'Metformin 500 MG Oral Tablet', 'Glucose [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma', 'Echocardiogram'.

name: cognitive assessment
title: cognitive assessment
description: A set of tests or assessments to determine congnitive abilities of a patient.

name: contextual conditioning behavior assay
title: contextual conditioning behavior assay
description: A behavioral assay devised to measure contextual conditioning behavior

name: distortion product otoacoustic emissions
title: distortion product otoacoustic emissions
description: DPOAE evaluates cochlear (outer hair cell) function and helps identify hearing loss due to cochlear dysfunction.Measures the sound waves generated by the inner ear (cochlea) in response to two simultaneous tones of different frequencies.  A probe with a microphone and speaker is placed in the ear canal to deliver sounds and capture the cochlear response.

name: elevated plus maze test
title: elevated plus maze test
description: A method which utilizes an experimental apparatus consisting of four arms, usually two enclosed and two open, in the shape of a plus (+) upon which the test animal can walk. The maze is elevated a set distance, for example 40 to 60 centimeters, above the floor or platform upon which the apparatus sits.

name: feeding assay
title: feeding assay
description: A behavioral assay that measures feeding-related behaviors or responses in experimental organisms. This encompasses various specific feeding assay types including membrane feeding assays, food intake measurements, and feeding preference assays.
rop_description: When the full six-axis combination resolves to an existing LOINC code, populate it directly. Forge attempts auto-resolution from the six-axis tuple. Examples: '51969-4' (Genetic analysis report), '100124-9' (Genotyping for human disease panel), '75614-8' (Whole genome sequence report).

name: gait measurement
title: gait measurement
description: Quantification of some aspect of a person's gait such as rhythm, variability or step length
rop_description: Axis 1 (LOINC Component): the analyte or entity being measured. Examples: 'Glucose', 'Hemoglobin A1c', 'RNA expression', 'DNA methylation', 'Genotype call', 'Protein abundance', 'Microbial 16S rRNA'.

name: genotyping
title: genotyping
description: The determination of the DNA sequence of an individual.

name: grip strength
title: grip strength
description: Assessment of muscle strength that measures that force with which one holds or grasps.

name: hand-held dynamometry
title: hand-held dynamometry
description: A technique to assess isometric muscle strength using a hand-held dynamometer.

name: metabolic screening
title: metabolic screening
description: Metabolic screening is a medical assessment that involves analyzing specific markers in bodily fluids to detect and evaluate metabolic disorders or imbalances.

name: n-back task
title: n-back task
description: A task in which items (e.g., letters) are presented one at a time and participants must identify each item that repeats relative to the item that occurred "n" items before its onset.

name: neuropsychological assessment
title: neuropsychological assessment
description: Evaluation for cognitive impairment by assessing the neuropsychological domains.

name: novelty response behavior assay
title: novelty response behavior assay
description: A behavioral assay devised to measure novelty response behavior

name: open field test
title: open field test
description: A test utilizing an enclosure consisting of a single area bordered by clear or opaque walls, used to measure movement, exploratory behavior, etc of an experimental subject.

name: optokinetic reflex assay
title: optokinetic reflex assay
description: The optokinetic reflex (OKR), which serves to stabilize a moving image on the retina, is a behavioral response that has many favorable attributes as a test of CNS function. The OKR requires no training, assesses the function of diverse CNS circuits, can be induced repeatedly with minimal fatigue or adaptation, and produces an electronic record that is readily and objectively quantifiable.

name: pattern electroretinogram
title: pattern electroretinogram
description: Pattern electroretinogram (PERG) is an electrophysiologic ophthalmologic test that provides non-invasive objective, quantitative measurement of central retinal function. PERG is the retinal response to a pattern-reversing, black-and-white checkerboard or stripped stimulus. The PERG assesses both macular and retinal ganglion cell electrical activity and can help differentiate between diseases of macular versus optic nerve dysfunction.

name: polysomnography
title: polysomnography
description: A group of tests (usually central electroencephalogram (EEG) (C3 or C4), reference occipital EEG (O1 or O2), right and left electro-oculogram (EOG), mental or submental electromylogram (EMG), thoracic effort, abdominal effort, nasal and oral airflow, a microphone to record snoring, pulse oxygen saturation, EKG, and video recording to document body positions during sleep) taken during sleep.

name: pure tone average
title: pure tone average
description: Metric used to summarize hearing sensitivity at specific frequencies to assess hearing impairment. As PTA increases, hearing ability decreases.

name: questionnaire
title: questionnaire
description: A document with a set of printed or written questions with a choice of answers, devised for the purposes of a survey or statistical study.

name: rotarod performance test
title: rotarod performance test
description: A method that utilizes a device consisting of a rotating rod the speed of which is mechanically driven and precisely controlled, for instance held constant or accelerated. The rotarod test is often used to measure riding time or endurance in order to evaluate balance, motor coordination or grip strength.

title: scale

name: six-minute walk test
title: six-minute walk test
description: The six-minute walk test (6MWT) is a simple, standardized functional assay of exercise capacity that measures how far the human subject can walk within a 6-minute duration; used to to evaluate lung and heart conditions.

name: weight

name: survival

name: word recognition score
title: word recognition score
description: Assays how well a person can understand speech by repeating a list of words. The WRS is a percentage of words correctly repeated. It's a more functional measure because it predicts whether hearing loss can be improved with amplification.

name: 2D AlamarBlue absorbance
title: 2D AlamarBlue absorbance
description: Cell viability assay based on detection of AlamarBlue (resazurin). Living cells reduce blue, non-fluorescent resazurin to the red, fluorescent molecule resorufin.  The amount of fluorescence or absorbance is proportional to the number of living cells and corresponds to the cell’s metabolic activity. For the absorbance-based assay, absorbance can be read at 570 nm.

name: 2D AlamarBlue fluorescence
title: 2D AlamarBlue fluorescence
description: Cell viability assay based on detection of AlamarBlue (resazurin). Living cells reduce blue, non-fluorescent resazurin to the red, fluorescent molecule resorufin. The amount of fluorescence or absorbance is proportional to the number of living cells and corresponds to the cell’s metabolic activity. For the fluorescence-based assay, color change and fluorescence can be detected using 560/590 nm (excitation/emission).

name: 3D microtissue viability
title: 3D microtissue viability
description: Cell viability assay on a 3D microtissue model.

name: ATPase activity assay
title: ATPase activity assay
description: As an assay used for drug discovery, measures enzymatic ATP hydrolysis to help assess levels of enzymatic activity induced by a candidate drug molecule.

name: BrdU proliferation assay
title: BrdU proliferation assay
description: A cell proliferation assay in which cells are cultured in the presence of BrdU which is incorporated into newly synthesized DNA of replicating cells (during the S phase of the cell cycle).

name: ELISA
title: ELISA
description: A highly sensitive technique for detecting and measuring antigens or antibodies in a solution; the solution is run over a surface to which immobilized antibodies specific to the substance have been attached, and if the substance is present, it will bind to the antibody layer, and its presence is verified and visualized with an application of antibodies that have been tagged in some way.

name: EdU proliferation assay
title: EdU proliferation assay
description: Proliferation assay using EdU as a thymidine nucleoside analog is an alternative to the classical BrdU assay.

name: FLIPR high-throughput cellular screening
title: FLIPR high-throughput cellular screening
description: An imaging-based system that allows measurement and analysis of peaks of human-derived induced pluripotent stem cells (hiPSCs),  differentiated into cardiomyocytes and neurons,  up to 100 times per second and quickly cherry pick events such as Early-After-Depolarization-like events (EAD-like events), fibrillation, and irregular beating.

name: HPLC
title: HPLC
description: Frequently referred to simply as HPLC, this form of column chromatography is used frequently in biochemistry.  The analyte is forced through a column by liquid at high pressure, which decreases the time the separated components remain on the stationary phase.

name: Migration Assay
title: Migration Assay
description: An in vitro assay in which cultured cells are monitored and analyzed for their ability to move into an acellular area of a culture material.

name: TIDE

description: Tracking of Indels by Decomposition (TIDE) is a simple and accurate assay to precisely determine the spectrum and frequency of targeted mutations generated  in a pool of cells by genome editing tools such as CRISPR/Cas9, TALENs and ZFNs.

name: TriKinetics activity monitoring
title: TriKinetics activity monitoring
description: TriKinetics systems quantify animal movement over time, and can be used to measure circadian rhythm, sleep, longevity, social interaction, geotaxis, learning, phototaxis, and drug response in various species of flies, mosquitoes, bees, spiders, ants, cockroaches, beetles, moths, zooplankton, and fish, among others.

name: current clamp assay
title: current clamp assay
description: The current clamp technique records the membrane potential by injecting current into a cell through the recording electrode.  Unlike in the voltage clamp mode, where the membrane potential is held at a level determined by the experimenter, in "current clamp" mode the membrane potential is free to vary,  and the amplifier records whatever voltage the cell generates on its own or as a result of stimulation.  This technique is used to study how a cell responds when electric current enters a cell; this is important for instance for understanding how neurons respond to neurotransmitters that act by opening membrane ion channels.

name: differential scanning calorimetry
title: differential scanning calorimetry
description: Differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) is the measurement of thermodynamic parameters (e.g. enthalpy) during a chemical or biochemical reaction of both a sample and a reference, by the known variation (step-wise or linear) of one variable, whilst a second is kept constant.

name: dynamic light scattering
title: dynamic light scattering
description: Dynamic Light Scattering (DLS), also known as photocorrelation spectroscopy, is a method for determining the size distribution of a sample of small particles in solution by illuminating the sample with a light source (usually a laser) and measuring the time-dependent fluctuations in the intensity of the scattered light caused by Brownian motion.

name: sandwich ELISA
title: sandwich ELISA
description: The sandwich ELISA measures the amount of antigen between two layers of antibodies. The antigens to be measured must contain at least two antigenic sites, capable of binding to antibody, since at least two antibodies act in the sandwich. So sandwich assays are restricted to the quantitation of multivalent antigens such as proteins or polysaccharides. Sandwich ELISAs for quantitation of antigens are especially valuable when the concentration of antigens is low and/or they are contained in high concentrations of contaminating protein.

rop_description: Preferred concept name in the standard vocabulary (OMOP concept.concept_name). Examples: 'Type 2 diabetes mellitus', 'Metformin 500 MG Oral Tablet', 'Glucose [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma', 'Echocardiogram'.

name: gene function
title: gene function
description: A term that expresses the function of a gene product.

name: gene symbol
title: gene symbol
description: A unique gene name approved by an organism specific nomenclature committee.

name: experimentalData
title: experimentalData
description: Any file derived from or pertaining to a scientific experiment. Experimental data annotations should be applied, possibly disease-related.

name: metadata
title: metadata
description: Data about data, information that describes another set of data.
rop_description: When the resource is a cell-line catalog (analogous to iNDI), structured metadata about the cell-line collection. JSON object with parental_line, genome_assembly, distribution_route, and per-line counts. Captures the iNDI-style 'mostly-constant fields' (KOLF2.1J parental, GRCh38, JAX distribution) without forcing them into per-row anchors.

name: protocol
title: protocol
description: A plan specification which has sufficient level of detail and quantitative information to communicate it between investigation agents, so that different investigation agents will reliably be able to independently reproduce the process.
rop_description: Identifier or DOI of the SOP / protocol used to process the biosample. Examples: PPMI BioFIND SOP v3.2, GP2 standard genotyping protocol, custom DOI.

name: protocol
title: protocol
description: A plan specification which has sufficient level of detail and quantitative information to communicate it between investigation agents, so that different investigation agents will reliably be able to independently reproduce the process.
rop_description: Reference to the acquisition protocol document (DOI, URL, or institutional protocol ID). Many cohort consortia (PPMI, ADNI, GENFI, EU-AIMS) publish detailed acquisition protocols that downstream consumers cite when describing methods.

name: protocol
title: protocol
description: A plan specification which has sufficient level of detail and quantitative information to communicate it between investigation agents, so that different investigation agents will reliably be able to independently reproduce the process.
rop_description: Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Research Ethics Committee (REC) protocol number under which the data was collected and is being shared. Required for traceability when downstream users need to verify ethical approval scope.

name: report
title: report
description: A document assembled by an author for the purpose of providing information for the audience.  A report is the output of a documenting process and has the objective to be consumed by a specific audience.  Topic of the report is on something that has completed. A report is not a single figure.  Examples of reports are journal article, patent application, grant progress report, case report (not patient record).

name: result
title: result
description: Any file that reports data results. Examples include figures, presentations, analysis, etc.
rop_description: Axis 5 (LOINC Scale): the scale of result reported. LOINC-native values used unchanged.

name: tool
title: tool
description: Any file or link that represents a tool, model, or algorithm; the tool annotations could be applied

name: weblink
title: weblink
description: An address to another document or other resource on the World Wide Web.
rop_description: Person(s) who reviewed and curated this resource record. JSON array of curator entries with structured identity. Replaces CARD Catalog's free-text 'Reviewer' field that used slash-delimited initials (e.g., 'Mette/Pietro') for joint review.

name: weblink
title: weblink
description: An address to another document or other resource on the World Wide Web.
rop_description: Full, human-readable name of the resource. Examples: 'Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative', 'Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative', 'Accelerating Medicines Partnership Parkinson Disease Knowledge Platform'.

name: weblink
title: weblink
description: An address to another document or other resource on the World Wide Web.
rop_description: Primary access URL for the resource. The canonical landing page. Additional URLs (alternative portals, archived snapshots, region-specific mirrors) go in ResourceAlternativeURLs.

name: workflow report
title: workflow report
description: Workflow-generated reports of analysis of primary data, usually created programmatically at completion of workflow step.

name: 129-Nf1<tm1Fcr>/Nci
title: 129-Nf1<tm1Fcr>/Nci
description: Nf1-/- embryos die between E11-E13.5 with heart defects and hyperplasia of the superior ganglia. Heterozygotes have an increased predisposition to pheochromocytoma and myeloid leukemia between 18 and 28 months of age. Heterozygotes also exhibit features of the learning deficits associated with NF1 patients, which has been shown to be associated with overactive Ras function

name: B6.129(Cg)-Nf1tm1Par/J
title: B6.129(Cg)-Nf1tm1Par/J
description: [From JAX]: These mice possess loxP sites flanking exons 31-32 of the neurofibromatosis 1 (Nf1) gene and have applications in studies of cancer, neural crest development and neurofibromatosis type I.

name: B6.129S1-Nf1tm1Cbr/J
title: B6.129S1-Nf1tm1Cbr/J
description: [From JAX]: Mice homozygous for this targeted allele (Nf123a-/-) lack the alternatively spliced exon 23a (which modifies the GTPase-activating protein (GAP) domain of Nf1). These mutant mice may be useful for neurological studies of Neurofibromatosis Type I, growth, differentiation, and learning and memory.

name: B6.129S2-Nf1tm1Tyj/J
title: B6.129S2-Nf1tm1Tyj/J
description: [From JAX]: Heterozygous animals do not exhibit the classical symptoms of Human neurofibromatosis type 1, but are highly predisposed to the formation of various tumor types, notably phaeochromocytoma, a tumor of the neural crest-derived adrenal medulla, and myeloid leukemia. Homozygosity leads to abnormal cardiac development and mid-gestational embryonic lethality. This strain may be useful in studies of cancer and developmental biology.

name: B6.129S6-Nf1<tm1Fcr>/Nci
title: B6.129S6-Nf1<tm1Fcr>/Nci
description: Nf1-/- embryos die between E11-E13.5 with heart defects and hyperplasia of the superior ganglia. Heterozygotes have an increased predisposition to pheochromocytoma and myeloid leukemia between 18 and 28 months of age. Heterozygotes also exhibit features of the learning deficits associated with NF1 patients, which has been shown to be associated with overactive Ras function

title: B6.129S6-Nf1tm1Fcr/J
description: [From GFF:] GEM mouse in which Exon 31 of Nf1 has been disrupted in one allele

name: B6;129-Trp53tm1Tyj Nf1tm1Tyj Suz12Gt(Betageo)1Khe/KcichJ
title: B6;129-Trp53tm1Tyj Nf1tm1Tyj Suz12Gt(Betageo)1Khe/KcichJ
description: [From JAX]: These NPS+/- mice carry Trp53, Nf1, and Suz12 mutations on chromosome 11 (in cis) and develop high-grade gliomas and malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors by approximately 3.5 months. They are suitable for use in applications related to the study of neurofibromatosis type 1 and tumor suppressor genes.

name: B6;129S2-Trp53tm1Tyj Nf1tm1Tyj/J
title: B6;129S2-Trp53tm1Tyj Nf1tm1Tyj/J
description: [From JAX]: These mice carry Trp53 and Nf1 targeted mutations on the same homolog of chromosome 11 (in cis). These mutations are approximately 10 Mbp apart and may segregate independently of one another. Double heterozygotes survive an average of five months and exhibit a significant increase in the percentage of soft tissue sarcomas. About 30% of tumors from the Nf1/Trp53 cis animals stain positively for S100 (consistent with glial cell origin) and exhibit classic histological features of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors (MPNSTs). This strain may be useful in studies of astrocytomas/glioblastomas and tumor suppressor genes.

name: B6;CBA-Nf1<em1Flmg>/Flmg
title: B6;CBA-Nf1<em1Flmg>/Flmg
description: Mice with a mutated (possibly truncated) Nf1 protein were generated using CRISPR/Cas9 technology. The mutated protein is the result of a single nucleotide deletion that takes place at exon 38 close to the targeted mutated R1809C position. A guide sgRNA along with the wild-type Cas9 protein were introduced into mouse zygotes via pronuclear microinjections.

name: dorsolateral prefrontal cortex
title: dorsolateral prefrontal cortex
description: The dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (DLPFC or DL-PFC) is an area in the prefrontal cortex of the primate brain.  It is one of the most recently derived parts of the human brain. It undergoes a prolonged period of maturation which lasts into adulthood.

name: groin
title: groin
description: Anatomically also known as the inguinal part of abdomen.
aliases: groin area|groin region|iliac fossa viewed surgically|iliac region|inguen|inguinal region

name: head
title: head
description: The head is the anterior-most division of the body.
rop_description: Aliquot volume in microliters. Null for solid tissue specimens or when not applicable.

name: occcipital lobe
title: occcipital lobe
description: The OCC is one of the four major lobes of the cerebral cortex and the visual processing hub.

name: thoracic spine
title: thoracic spine
aliases: axial skeleton thoracic region|columna vertebralis thoracicus|thoracic vertebrae series|thoracic vertebral column

name: adrenal gland
title: adrenal gland
description: Either of a pair of complex endocrine organs near the anterior medial border of the kidney consisting of a mesodermal cortex that produces glucocorticoid, mineralocorticoid, and androgenic hormones and an ectodermal medulla that produces epinephrine and norepinephrine.

name: Bursa Of Fabricius
title: Bursa Of Fabricius
description: An epithelial outgrowth of lymphoid tissue in the cloaca of young birds, responsible for B-lymphocyte maturation. It undergoes atrophy and involution at six months of age.

name: blood
title: blood
description: The fluid that circulates in the heart, arteries, capillaries, and veins of a vertebrate animal carrying nourishment and oxygen to and bringing away waste products from all parts of the body.

name: bone marrow
title: bone marrow
description: The soft, fatty, vascular tissue that fills most bone cavities and is the source of red blood cells and many white blood cells.

name: brain
title: brain
description: The portion of the vertebrate central nervous system that constitutes the organ of thought and neural coordination.

name: breast
title: breast
description: The fore or ventral part of the body between the neck and the abdomen.

name: colon
title: colon
description: The part of the large intestine that extends from the cecum to the rectum.

name: eye
title: eye
description: The organ of sight or vision. [ NCI ]

name: kidney
title: kidney
description: One of the two bean-shaped organs located on each side of the spine in the retroperitoneum.
aliases: Kidney structure (body structure)|Kidney, NOS|Urinary System, Kidney

name: lung
title: lung
description: One of the usually paired compound saccular thoracic organs that constitute the basic respiratory organ of air-breathing vertebrates.

name: lymph node
title: lymph node
description: A bean-shaped organ surrounded by a connective tissue capsule.
aliases: Lymph Gland|Lymph Node, NOS|Lymphatic Gland|Reticuloendothelial System, Lymph Node|Structure of lymph node (body structure)

name: mammary gland
title: mammary gland
description: A specialized accessory gland of the skin of mammals that secretes milk. The gland is typically only developed in females, and regresses in males.

name: inner ear
title: inner ear
description: The innermost part of the vertebrate ear that is responsible for sound sensation and balance, containing the cochlea and vestibular apparatus.

name: nerves
title: nerves
description: Any of the filamentous bands of nervous tissue that connect parts of the nervous system with the other organs, conduct nervous impulses, and are made up of axons and dendrites together with protective and supportive structures.

name: nose
title: nose
description: A structure of special sense serving as an organ of the sense of smell and as an entrance to the respiratory tract.

name: ovary
title: ovary
description: One of the typically paired essential female reproductive organs that produce eggs and in vertebrates female sex hormones.

name: pancreas
title: pancreas
description: A large lobulated gland of vertebrates that secretes digestive enzymes and the hormones insulin and glucagon.

name: placenta
title: placenta
description: The vascular organ in mammals except monotremes and marsupials that unites the fetus to the maternal uterus and mediates its metabolic exchanges through a more or less intimate association of uterine mucosal with chorionic and usually allantoic tissues.

name: prostate
title: prostate
description: Lobular organ the parenchyma of which has as its parts glandular acini which are continuous with the prostatic part of the urethra.

name: skin
title: skin
description: The integument of an animal (as a fur-bearing mammal or a bird) separated from the body usually with its hair or feathers.

name: spleen
title: spleen
description: A highly vascular ductless organ that is located in the left abdominal region near the stomach or intestine of most vertebrates and is concerned with final destruction of red blood cells, filtration and storage of blood, and production of lymphocytes.

name: smooth muscle
title: smooth muscle
description: Muscle tissue that lacks cross striations, that is made up of elongated spindle-shaped cells having a central nucleus, and that is found in vertebrate visceral structures (as the stomach and bladder) as thin sheets performing functions not subject to conscious control by the mind and in all or most of the musculature of invertebrates other than arthropods.

name: tonsil
title: tonsil
description: The human palatine tonsils and the nasopharyngeal tonsil are lymphoepithelial tissues located in strategic areas of the oropharynx and nasopharynx, although most commonly, the term tonsils refers to the palatine tonsils that can be seen in the back of the throat.

name: thymus
title: thymus
description: A glandular structure of largely lymphoid tissue that functions especially in the development of the body's immune system, is present in the young of most vertebrates typically in the upper anterior chest or at the base of the neck, and tends to atrophy in the adult.

name: B-lymphocytes
title: B-lymphocytes
description: A lymphocyte of B lineage with the phenotype CD19-positive and surface immunoglobulin-positive.

name: CD8+ T-Cells
title: CD8+ T-Cells
description: Is a T lymphocyte (a type of white blood cell) that kills cancer cells, cells that are infected (particularly with viruses), or cells that are damaged in other ways.

name: CNON
title: CNON
description: Cultured Neuronal cells derived from Olfactory Neuroepithelium

name: DRG/nerve root neurosphere cell
title: DRG/nerve root neurosphere cell
description: Dorsal root ganglia/nerve root neurosphere cells (DNSCs) are cultured from embryonic DRGs/nerve roots and have been used in in vitro sphere assays to study the origin of para-spinal neurofibromas. See reference PMC4254535

name: Embryonic stem cells
title: Embryonic stem cells
description: Embryonic stem (ES) cells are cells derived from the inner cell mass of the early embryo that can be propagated indefinitely in the primitive undifferentiated state while remaining pluripotent.

name: GABAergic neurons
title: GABAergic neurons
description: A neuron that uses GABA as a vesicular neurotransmitter.

name: GLUtamatergic neurons
title: GLUtamatergic neurons
description: Have Glutamate receptors, which are synaptic receptors located primarily on the membranes of neuronal cells. Glutamate (the conjugate base of glutamic acid) is abundant in the human body, but particularly in the nervous system and especially prominent in the human brain.

name: NeuN+
title: NeuN+
description: Is a neuronal nuclear antigen that is commonly used as a biomarker for neurons and NeuN immunoreactivity has been widely used to identify neurons in tissue culture to measure the neuron(positive)/glia(negative) ratio in brain regions.

name: NeuN-
title: NeuN-
description: Is a neuronal nuclear antigen that is commonly used as a biomarker for neurons and NeuN immunoreactivity has been widely used to identify neurons in tissue culture to measure the neuron(positive)/glia(negative) ratio in brain regions.

name: SH-SY5Y
title: SH-SY5Y
description: Human neuroblastoma clonal subline of the neuroepithelioma cell line SK-N-SH that had been established in 1970 from the bone marrow biopsy of a 4-year-old girl with metastatic neuroblastoma.

name: Schwann cell precursor
title: Schwann cell precursor
description: A giioblast cell that develops from a migratory neural crest cell. The SCP is embedded among neurons (axons) with minimal extracellular spaces separating them from nerve cell membranes and has no basal lamina. In rodents SCPs are the only cells in the Schwann cell linage that expresses Cdh19.

name: arachnoid
title: arachnoid
description: An arachnoid mater is a delicate membrane that encloses the spinal cord and brain and lies between the pia mater and dura mater.

name: astrocytes
title: astrocytes
description: Astrocytes (from 'star' cells) are irregularly shaped with many long processes, including those with end-feet which form the glial (limiting) membrane and directly and indirectly contribute to the blood-brain barrier.

name: cultured Muller glia
title: cultured Muller glia
description: Astrocyte-like radial glial cell that extends vertically throughout the retina, with the nucleus are usually in the middle of the inner nuclear layer. [ http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21911394 GOC : NV ]

name: epithelial
title: epithelial
description: Somatic cells that cover the surface of the body and line its cavities.

name: epithelial-like
title: epithelial-like
description: In cell morphology, epithelial-like cells are polygonal in shape with more regular dimensions, and grow attached to a substrate in discrete patches.

name: fibroblast
title: fibroblast
description: A connective tissue cell which secretes an extracellular matrix rich in collagen and other macromolecules. Flattened and irregular in outline with branching processes; appear fusiform or spindle-shaped.

name: iPSC
title: iPSC
description: Induced pluripotent stem cells (iPS cells or iPSCs) are a type of pluripotent stem cell artificially derived from a non-pluripotent cell.

name: iPSC-derived telencephalic organoids
title: iPSC-derived telencephalic organoids
description: three-dimensional neural cultures (organoids) derived from induced pluripotent stem cells.

name: lymphoblast
title: lymphoblast
description: Often referred to as a blast cell. Unlike other usages of the suffix -blast a lymphoblast is a further differentiation of a lymphocyte, T- or B-, occasioned by an antigenic stimulus. The lymphoblast usually develops by enlargement of a lymphocyte, active re-entry to the S phase of the cell cycle, mitogenesis and production of much m-RNA and ribosomes.

name: macrophages
title: macrophages
description: A mononuclear phagocyte present in variety of tissues, typically differentiated from monocytes, capable of phagocytosing a variety of extracellular particulate material, including immune complexes, microorganisms, and dead cells.

name: meningioma
title: meningioma
description: A central nervous system cancer tissue that are manifested in the central nervous system and arise from the arachnoid 'cap' cells of the arachnoid villi in the meninges.

name: microglia
title: microglia
description: The small, non-neural, interstitial cells of mesodermal origin that form part of the supporting structure of the central nervous system.

name: monocytes
title: monocytes
description: Myeloid mononuclear recirculating leukocyte that can act as a precursor of tissue macrophages, osteoclasts and some populations of tissue dendritic cells.

name: oligodendrocyte
title: oligodendrocyte
description: A class of large neuroglial (macroglial) cells in the central nervous system. Form the insulating myelin sheath of axons in the central nervous system.

name: round
title: round
description: A phenotype observation at the level of the cell shape where the cell is round

name: schwann
title: schwann
description: Schwann cells are a variety of glial cell that keep peripheral nerve fibres (both myelinated and unmyelinated) alive.

name: schwannoma
title: schwannoma
description: A neoplasm that arises from SCHWANN CELLS of the cranial, peripheral, and autonomic nerves.

name: teratoma
title: teratoma
description: A non-seminomatous germ cell tumor characterized by the presence of various tissues which correspond to the different germinal layers (endoderm, mesoderm, and ectoderm). It occurs in the testis, ovary, and extragonadal sites including central nervous system, mediastinum, lung, and stomach

name: SIGTR ES cell line AA0320
title: SIGTR ES cell line AA0320
description: High throughput gene trapping was performed by inserting a gene trap vector containing a splice-acceptor sequence upstream of a reporter gene, β-geo (a fusion of β-galactosidase and neomycin phosphotransferase II), into an intronic or coding region of genomic DNA. The resulting insertional mutation creates a fusion transcript containing sequences from exons upstream of the insertion joined to the β-geo marker, allowing cell lines where the vector has successfully interrupted a gene to be identified.

name: 22q-related schwannomatosis
title: 22q-related schwannomatosis
description: A schwannomatosis that causes a predisposition to develop multiple schwannoma. It is diagnosed when an individual does not meet criteria for NF2-related schwannomatosis, SMARCB1-related schwannomatosis, or LTZR1-related schwannomatosis and both of the following molecular features exist: a loss of heterozygosity (LOH) of the same chromosome 22q markers in two anatomically distinct tumors or hybrid nerve sheath tumors and a different NF2 pathogenic variant in each tumor which cannot be detected in unaffected tissue.

Individuals with the disorder have increased susceptibility to the development of benign and malignant tumors.

With striking presentation and  severe dysmorphism, massive soft tissue neurofibroma is also labeled elephantiasis neuromatosa. Although considered benign it  shares similarities with malignant peripheral nerve sheath  tumors such as local infiltration and cell mitogenesis. Massive soft tissue neurofibroma is associated with significant morbidity and mortality and almost no response to  conventional chemotherapy and radiotherapy. Surgery is eventually pursued, although bleeding may be a life-threatening  complication as massive soft tissue neurofibromas are highly  vascularized.

name: Melanoma
title: Melanoma
description: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain. [ NCI ]

name: fileFormat

rop_description: Specific format version of the asset. Critical for downstream parser compatibility — VCFv4.1 and VCFv4.2 differ in non-trivial ways, BAM 1.6 introduced changes that pre-1.6 readers don't handle, AnnData minor versions add fields. Examples: 'VCFv4.2', 'BAMv1.6', 'CRAM-3.1', 'AnnData-0.10', 'DICOM-3.0', 'NIfTI-1.1', 'Parquet-2.6', 'MatrixMarket-2.0'.

rop_description: Reference to the canonical pedigree file representation when one exists. Format: PLINK FAM (.fam), GEDCOM (.ged), CSV with documented columns, or PED format. Allows downstream tools that prefer file-based pedigree input to retrieve the authoritative version rather than reconstructing from per-row anchors. Same URI scheme rules as Tier 8 DataAssetURI (DRS preferred).

name: fileSize
title: File Size
description: Size of file in bytes.
rop_description: Statistical disclosure control threshold: the minimum cell size below which any published summary statistic, frequency table, or aggregate must be suppressed. When set to N, no published bin/cell may contain fewer than N participants. Counts of zero are reported as zero (not suppressed). Complementary cells must be suppressed if their suppression would allow inference of a small cell — this is captured in SmallCellSuppressionRule. Common values: 5 (UK Biobank, common minimum), 10 (NIH dbGaP, NACC), 11 (CMS), 20 (All of Us, gnomAD).

When data crosses cohort boundaries, this field should be populated with a pseudonymized derivative (e.g., HMAC of the cohort-native identifier under a per-recipient key) rather than the raw cohort identifier; the linkage to the raw identifier is preserved in IndividualAliases under appropriate access controls.

Stable across the lifetime of the cohort. PLINK FAM file Column 1 maps directly to this field.

JSON object with parental_line, genome_assembly, distribution_route, and per-line counts. Captures the iNDI-style 'mostly-constant fields' (KOLF2.1J parental, GRCh38, JAX distribution) without forcing them into per-row anchors.

rop_description: Globally unique identifier for an individual participant within the source cohort. Multiple biosamples, visits, and observations may be nested under one IndividualID. Stability is required across the lifetime of the cohort. Format and generation are cohort-defined; uniqueness must hold within the cohort. When data crosses cohort boundaries, this field should be populated with a pseudonymized derivative (e.g., HMAC of the cohort-native identifier under a per-recipient key) rather than the raw cohort identifier; the linkage to the raw identifier is preserved in IndividualAliases under appropriate access controls.

name: isMultiIndividual
title: Is Multi-Individual
description: Whether or not a file has data for multiple individuals (Yes; No)
rop_description: Cross-cohort and cross-system alias mapping for the same individual, supporting federated identification across sparse or distributed datasets. JSON array of {authority, identifier, linkage_method, linkage_confidence} entries. Example: a participant enrolled in PPMI as 'PPMI-3001', re-enrolled in GP2 as 'GP2-C0042-2031', and linked to UK Biobank EID '1234567' via probabilistic broker matching has three IndividualAliases entries with appropriate confidence scores. The alias map is loaded only in environments holding the appropriate Data Use Agreements; analyses that do not require federation operate on IndividualID alone.

name: isMultiIndividual
title: Is Multi-Individual
description: Whether or not a file has data for multiple individuals (Yes; No)
rop_description: Structured array of external dataset and repository linkages for this individual, aligned with GA4GH service standards. Each entry describes one repository where the individual's data is accessible, the GA4GH service types offered (DRS, Beacon v2, Data Connect, Phenopackets, TES, WES, Service Registry), the appropriate endpoints, authentication requirements, and data scope available there. Together with IndividualAliases (cross-cohort identifiers) and Tier 8 DataAssetURI (specific files), this anchor closes the federation loop: a downstream consumer holding one IndividualID can mechanically resolve every repository where that individual's data lives, what services those repositories expose, what authentication is required, and what data scope is available — without having to read tribal documentation or email PIs.

name: isMultiSpecimen
title: Is Multi-Specimen
description: Whether or not a file has data for multiple specimens (Yes; No)
rop_description: Globally unique identifier for the biosample within the cohort. Must be stable, non-PHI, and traceable to the consenting participant via BiosampleSubjectID.

name: isMultiSpecimen

name: isMultiSpecimen

description: Whether or not a file has data for multiple specimens (Yes; No)
rop_description: Axis 4 (LOINC System): the specimen/system measured. Bridges to RoP biosample anchors rather than duplicating that vocabulary — value should resolve to the BiosampleID and inherit its SampleTypeControlled.

name: isPairedEnd

description: (Legacy/deprecated annotation) Whether or not is paired-end sequencing (Yes; No).

name: isPrimaryCell

description: Whether or not cellType is primary (Yes; No)
rop_description: DOI of the primary validation publication. Critical for reproducibility — analyses citing instrument scores should cite the validation paper, not just the instrument name. Examples: MoCA → Nasreddine et al 2005 (10.1111/j.1532-5415.2005.53221.x), MDS-UPDRS → Goetz et al 2008 (10.1002/mds.22340).

name: isPrimaryCell

rop_description: Primary Data Use Ontology (DUO) consent class. The fundamental scope of permitted research use granted by the participant under the consent form they signed. RoP uses GA4GH's Data Use Ontology as the authoritative vocabulary for consent classes. Restrictions and modifiers (non-commercial, IRB-required, time-limited, etc.) are recorded separately in ConsentDUORestrictions.

name: isPrimaryCell

description: Whether or not cellType is primary (Yes; No)

name: panelVersion

rop_description: Version string for PipelineSoftware. Required for reproducibility.

name: panelVersion

Software versions periodically introduce reconstruction algorithm changes that produce subtle quantitative differences — recording the version supports reproducibility audits.

name: interactionScore

rop_description: What the collection covers. JSON object with disease areas, modalities covered, target cohorts, and explicit out-of-scope items. Helps downstream consumers decide whether the collection is appropriate for their use case.

name: interactionScore

description: Quantitative confidence or interaction score reported for the protein interaction (e.g., SAINT score, % control).
rop_description: 0-9 FAIR score for the collection itself (carries CARD methodology to the collection level). Distinct from per-resource FAIR scores in Module 12 — a collection of FAIR-compliant resources can still score poorly on its own meta-FAIR if the collection lacks versioning, persistent identifiers, or documentation.

name: interactionScoreType

rop_description: Structured array of external dataset and repository linkages for this individual, aligned with GA4GH service standards. Each entry describes one repository where the individual's data is accessible, the GA4GH service types offered (DRS, Beacon v2, Data Connect, Phenopackets, TES, WES, Service Registry), the appropriate endpoints, authentication requirements, and data scope available there. Together with IndividualAliases (cross-cohort identifiers) and Tier 8 DataAssetURI (specific files), this anchor closes the federation loop: a downstream consumer holding one IndividualID can mechanically resolve every repository where that individual's data lives, what services those repositories expose, what authentication is required, and what data scope is available — without having to read tribal documentation or email PIs.

name: studyStatus
title: Study Status
description: Status of a study.
rop_description: Whether biological paternity information is known. True when paternity is established; false when paternity is unknown; 'in-cohort' when known and the father is also enrolled (PaternalIndividualID populated); 'out-of-cohort' when known but father is not enrolled. Disambiguates the common cause of null PaternalIndividualID values.

name: studyStatus
title: Study Status
description: Status of a study.
rop_description: Whether biological maternity information is known. Same semantics as PaternalKnown.

name: studyStatus
title: Study Status
description: Status of a study.
rop_description: Whether this individual is a founder of the pedigree (no parents in the dataset). Founders contribute genetic material that is not derived from other dataset members. Required for variance-component models, IBD inference, and many family-based association methods. Computed automatically when PaternalIndividualID and MaternalIndividualID are known but recorded explicitly to avoid recomputation by every downstream tool.

rop_description: Free-text description of the resource — what it is, what data it produces, what its intended use is. One paragraph recommended.

name: summary
title: Summary
description: A short description (an abstract).
rop_description: Statistical disclosure control threshold: the minimum cell size below which any published summary statistic, frequency table, or aggregate must be suppressed. When set to N, no published bin/cell may contain fewer than N participants. Counts of zero are reported as zero (not suppressed). Complementary cells must be suppressed if their suppression would allow inference of a small cell — this is captured in SmallCellSuppressionRule. Common values: 5 (UK Biobank, common minimum), 10 (NIH dbGaP, NACC), 11 (CMS), 20 (All of Us, gnomAD).

name: summary
title: Summary
description: A short description (an abstract).
rop_description: Category of summary statistics dataset. Determines which downstream tools and parsers apply, what columns to expect in the underlying file, and which meta-analysis methods are appropriate.

name: targetCaptureKitID

description: A unique identifier for the kit used to construct a genomic library using target capture-based techniques, composed of the vendor name, kit name and kit version.

name: targetDepth
title: Target Depth
description: The targeted read depth prior to sequencing.
rop_description: Axis 1 (LOINC Component): the analyte or entity being measured. Examples: 'Glucose', 'Hemoglobin A1c', 'RNA expression', 'DNA methylation', 'Genotype call', 'Protein abundance', 'Microbial 16S rRNA'.

name: timepointUnit

name: totalReads
title: Total Reads
description: If available, the total number of reads collected from samtools.
rop_description: PET tracer injected dose in megabecquerels (MBq). DICOM tag (0018,1074) RadionuclideTotalDose. Required for SUV computation. Combined with subject body weight (Theme 12 measurement) and injection-to-scan time interval, enables SUV / SUVR derivation.

name: totalReads
title: Total Reads
description: If available, the total number of reads collected from samtools.
rop_description: Plausibility bounds for the instrument's total score. JSON object {min, max, valid_increments}. Used for outlier detection and unit-error catching: a MoCA score of 38 is impossible (max=30), a GDS-15 score of 25 is impossible (max=15). Not clinical reference ranges (those are policy/locality-specific) — just plausibility for catching data errors.

name: totalReads
title: Total Reads
description: If available, the total number of reads collected from samtools.
rop_description: Total effective sample size for the summary statistics. For meta-analyses, this is the sum across contributing cohorts. For QTL studies, this is the per-tissue sample size. Critical for power calculations and meta-analysis weighting.
